overview: Startup India Initiative is a flagship Government of India program launched in 2016 by DPIIT to foster innovation, support entrepreneurs, and drive sustainable economic growth. It provides DPIIT recognition to eligible startups, enabling access to tax benefits, easier compliance, IPR fast-tracking, funding support, and other incentives. The initiative encompasses multiple schemes including the Fund of Funds, Seed Fund Scheme, Credit Guarantee Scheme, and various incubation and mentorship programs.
target_beneficiaries: startups; entrepreneurs; MSME; women entrepreneurs; agri-startups; Deep Tech Startups
eligibility: An entity must be incorporated or registered in India as a private limited company (as defined in the Companies Act, 2013) or registered as a partnership firm (registered under section 59 of the Partnership Act, 1932) or a limited liability partnership (under the Limited Liability Partnership Act, 2008) or a Multi-State Cooperative Society registered with the Central Registrar of Cooperative Societies (under the Multi-State Cooperative Societies Act, 2002) or a Cooperative Society registered under any State or Union Territory Cooperative Societies Act with the respective Registrar of Cooperative Societies in India; must be within a period of ten years from the date of its incorporation or registration; must have a turnover for any of the financial years since incorporation or registration not exceeding two hundred crore rupees; and must be working towards innovation, development or improvement of products or processes or services, or is a scalable business model with a high potential of employment generation or wealth creation. Provided that, an entity formed by splitting up or reconstruction of an existing business shall not be considered a Startup. For Deep Tech Startups, the period is up to twenty years and the turnover limit is three hundred crore rupees.
benefits: DPIIT recognised startups can access tax exemptions under Section 80-IAC (100% tax exemption on profits for three financial years), exemption under Section 56(2)(VIIB) (Angel Tax), self-certification under 9 labour and environment laws, fast-tracking of patent applications with 80% rebate in fees, relaxed norms in public procurement (exemption from prior experience/turnover criteria and Earnest Money Deposit), faster exit provisions (winding up within 90 days), access to National Funding Schemes (Fund of Funds, Seed Fund Scheme, Credit Guarantee Scheme), access to private funding avenues, ecosystem development initiatives (National Startup Awards, BHASKAR, MAARG mentorship platform), and State/Union Territory incentives.
financial_support: Funding support is provided through the Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) with a total corpus of INR 10,000 crore, managed by SIDBI, which invests in SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) that in turn fund startups. The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialisation. The Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS) provides credit guarantee up to ₹20 crore per borrower to facilitate collateral-free debt funding from Member Institutions. Additionally, the initiative facilitates access to government loan schemes, investor connect platforms, and various grant-based programs.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to INR 20 Lakhs from SISFS for early-stage validation
application_process: 1. Register on the Startup India portal (www.startupindia.gov.in). 2. Apply for DPIIT recognition via the National Single Window System (NSWS) at www.nsws.gov.in by adding 'Registration as a Startup' to your dashboard. 3. Fill the startup application form with entity details, innovation description, and scalability aspects. 4. Upload required documents including Certificate of Incorporation/Registration, PAN, Memorandum of Association/LLP Deed, Board Resolution (if any), Annual Accounts, and Income Tax returns. 5. Submit the application and await approval (typically issued within 2 working days). 6. Upon recognition, access benefits via the Startup India portal and apply for specific schemes like tax exemptions, seed fund, or credit guarantee through respective portals.
deadlines: Rolling basis — no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted year-round.
implementing_agency: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry
